ogTitle: South Trees vs South Wellesley Islands: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in South Trees and South Wellesley Islands.
heading: South Trees vs South Wellesley Islands — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between South Trees, Queensland and South Wellesley Islands, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Wellesley Islands has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to South Trees (32/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is South Trees or South Wellesley Islands safer from flooding?
faq.answer: South Trees has a flood risk score of 60/100, while South Wellesley Islands scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: South Trees scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. South Wellesley Islands scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in South Trees or South Wellesley Islands?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Wellesley Islands has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.32% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
